description_long: This object is a weathered stone obelisk base, rectangular in shape with a flat top and a stepped plinth. The surface is marked by erosion and discoloration, with visible chipping and damage, especially on the upper edges and corners. The front face features a partially legible inscription, likely in Latin, carved into a recessed panel. The sides and back are plain, with only simple linear molding as decoration. The base sits on a slightly raised stone platform, with small patches of grass and gravel around its perimeter. The overall appearance suggests historical significance, possibly as a monument or grave marker.
description: A weathered rectangular stone obelisk base with a partially legible inscription and simple linear moldings, set on a raised platform with some grass and gravel around it.
description_view_0: Front view showing the recessed panel with a partially legible inscription and some chipping on the upper left edge.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the plain, undecorated surface and the stepped base, with visible weathering and discoloration.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a mostly plain surface with some chipping and a small hole near the bottom right corner.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing more pronounced chipping and erosion on the upper left, with the stepped base visible.
attribute_inscription: There is a partially legible inscription on the front face, carved in a serifed style, likely Latin. The text is weathered and difficult to read.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible beyond simple linear moldings framing the panels.
attribute_base_design: The base is rectangular with a stepped plinth, featuring simple linear moldings and a slightly raised platform. The stone is weathered and chipped in places.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and roughly finished, with significant chipping and no decorative features.
attribute_surrounding_features: Small patches of grass and gravel are present around the base.
